mappings.rationaleAI: Aztec civilization directly addresses the Aztec state (C.2) and culture (C.3).
Maya civilization covers Olmec developments (B.1.c, B.1.d), lowland Maya (B.2.a.ii), Late Classic Maya (B.2.c), and Toltec-Maya Post-Classic (C.1).
Spanish conquest connects to Montezuma II (C.2) and the Spanish conquest era (C.3).
Archaeological methods connect to sources (A) and Pre-Classic/Classic developments (B).
Prehistoric development connects to Late Pleistocene peoples (B.1.a).
Anthropological study from Mexico connects to Aztec culture (C.3).
Conquest narratives connect to Aztec culture at the conquest (C.3).
Native American mythology connects to the legend of Quetzalcoatl (C.1).
Indigenous peoples provide context for Meso-American ethnography (A).
